label: Role of the entity in relation to the AI system
description: The role of the natural or legal person in the AI system supply chain. A single entity may hold multiple roles for different systems (e.g., provider of one system and deployer of another).
source: Self-declaration; determined by nature of relationship to AI system

label: Risk category of the AI system

value_descriptions.prohibited: Practices explicitly banned under Art. 5 — e.g. subliminal manipulation, social scoring by public authorities, real-time remote biometric identification in public spaces (with narrow exceptions), emotion recognition in workplaces or schools.
value_descriptions.high_risk: AI systems posing significant risks to health, safety or fundamental rights. Includes safety components of Annex I regulated products (machinery, medical devices, vehicles) and use cases listed in Annex III (biometrics, critical infrastructure, employment, law enforcement, border control, justice).
value_descriptions.limited_risk_transparency: AI systems requiring transparency obligations only — e.g. chatbots interacting with users, systems generating synthetic content (deepfakes). No conformity assessment required.
value_descriptions.minimal_risk: AI systems with minimal or no risk (e.g. spam filters, AI in video games). No mandatory requirements under this Regulation.

description: The risk tier of the AI system as determined by Art. 5 (prohibited), Art. 6 (high-risk), Art. 50 (limited risk — transparency only), or no mandatory requirements (minimal risk). Determines the applicable obligation set.
source: Provider's classification assessment; confirmed by conformity assessment for high-risk systems

label: Basis for high-risk classification

value_descriptions.annex_i_product_safety_component: The AI system is a safety component of a product regulated under Annex I legislation (e.g. machinery, medical devices, vehicles, toys). Conformity assessment must align with the product's own procedure — third-party notified body assessment is typically required.
value_descriptions.annex_iii_use_case: The AI system falls within one of the 8 use case areas listed in Annex III. Most can use internal conformity assessment (Annex VI) unless it is a biometric identification system.
value_descriptions.both: The AI system is both a safety component of an Annex I product AND falls within an Annex III use case. The stricter Annex I product conformity procedure applies.
value_descriptions.not_high_risk: The AI system does not meet Annex I or Annex III criteria, or the Art. 6(3) exemption applies. High-risk obligations do not apply.

description: Whether the high-risk classification arises from Art. 6(1) (safety component of an Annex I product requiring 3rd-party conformity assessment) or Art. 6(2) (listed in Annex III use cases). Affects conformity assessment procedure and application deadlines.
source: Provider's classification assessment; product legislation check for Annex I

label: Annex III use case area (for high-risk classification under Art. 6(2))

value_descriptions.1_biometrics: Remote biometric identification systems, biometric categorisation inferring sensitive attributes (race, political views, religion, sexual orientation), emotion recognition systems.
value_descriptions.2_critical_infrastructure: AI used as safety components in critical infrastructure: electricity, water, gas, heating, transport, internet.
value_descriptions.3_education_vocational_training: AI determining access to educational institutions, evaluating learning outcomes, assessing students, monitoring during exams.
value_descriptions.4_employment_workers_management: AI for recruitment, CV screening, job advertisements, promotion decisions, task allocation, performance and behaviour monitoring.
value_descriptions.5_essential_services_benefits: AI for credit scoring, social benefits or services eligibility, emergency services dispatch, health and life insurance risk assessment.
value_descriptions.6_law_enforcement: AI for individual risk assessment in criminal investigations, polygraph-type tools, crime prediction, evidence reliability assessment, facial recognition from CCTV footage.
value_descriptions.7_migration_asylum_border: AI for visa or asylum application risk assessment, document authenticity verification, examining protection applications.
value_descriptions.8_administration_of_justice_democratic_p: AI assisting courts in fact-finding or applying the law, influencing elections or voting behaviour.
value_descriptions.none: The AI system does not fall within any Annex III use case. High-risk classification under Art. 6(2) does not apply.

description: The Annex III category that triggers high-risk classification. Area 1 includes: remote biometric identification systems, biometric categorisation inferring sensitive attributes, emotion recognition. Areas 2-8 cover infrastructure, education, employment, essential services, law enforcement, migration, justice/democracy.
source: Provider's classification assessment against Annex III use cases

label: Art. 6(3) exemption: AI system in Annex III but not high-risk
description: Despite being listed in Annex III, a system is NOT high-risk if it: (a) performs a narrow procedural task; (b) improves the result of a previously completed human activity; (c) detects decision-making patterns for human review without replacing human assessment; (d) performs a preparatory task. Exception: systems that profile natural persons are ALWAYS high-risk regardless of these conditions.
source: Provider's assessment; documented in technical documentation

label: Type of conformity assessment required

value_descriptions.internal_control: Annex VI procedure: the provider conducts the assessment internally and draws up the EU Declaration of Conformity. Available for most Annex III systems.
value_descriptions.third_party_notified_body: Annex VII procedure: an accredited notified body must conduct or oversee the assessment. Required for Annex I product safety components and real-time remote biometric identification systems.
value_descriptions.not_applicable: No conformity assessment required — the system is not high-risk.

description: Whether the high-risk AI system requires internal conformity assessment (Annex VI) or third-party assessment by a notified body (Annex VII). Systems in Annex I product legislation areas generally require notified body assessment; some Annex III systems allow internal assessment.
source: Determined by applicable conformity assessment procedure (Art. 43)

label: Deployer is a public authority or body
description: Whether the deployer is a public authority or body (including EU institutions). Public authority deployers of high-risk AI systems have additional obligations: register in EU database (Art. 26(8)) and conduct a Fundamental Rights Impact Assessment (Art. 27).
source: Entity classification

label: The AI system is a general-purpose AI model (GPAI model)
description: Whether the product is a GPAI model: an AI model displaying significant generality and capable of competently performing a wide range of distinct tasks, trained on large amounts of data using self-supervised learning, that can be integrated into AI systems or applications.
source: Provider's determination based on model characteristics

label: GPAI model is classified as having systemic risk
description: Whether the GPAI model has systemic risk, either by: (a) presumption — training compute > 10^25 floating point operations (FLOPs); (b) Commission designation based on high-impact capabilities. Systemic risk triggers additional obligations under Art. 55.
source: Provider's calculation of training compute; or Commission designation

threshold.note: Presumption of systemic risk if cumulative training compute exceeds this threshold. Commission may adjust threshold by delegated act.

label: GPAI model is released under a free and open-source licence
description: Whether the GPAI model is released under a free and open-source licence with publicly available parameters, architecture, and model card. Open-source GPAI models are exempt from Art. 53(1)(a)(b) documentation requirements UNLESS they have systemic risk.
source: Provider's licence terms

label: AI system directly interacts with natural persons
description: Whether the AI system is intended to interact directly with natural persons (chatbots, virtual assistants, etc.). Triggers Art. 50(1) disclosure obligation for providers.
source: Provider's determination based on intended purpose

label: AI system generates synthetic audio, image, video or text content
description: Whether the AI system generates synthetic content (deepfakes, AI-generated text, images, audio, video). Triggers Art. 50(2) machine-readable marking obligation for providers and Art. 50(4) disclosure obligation for deployers.
source: Provider's determination based on system outputs

label: AI system uses emotion recognition or biometric categorisation
description: Whether the AI system uses emotion recognition (inferring emotional states) or biometric categorisation (assigning persons to categories based on biometric data). Triggers Art. 50(3) disclosure obligation for deployers.
source: Provider's determination in technical documentation / instructions for use

label: Deployer uses the AI system in an employment or work context
description: Whether the high-risk AI system is used in an employment context affecting workers. Triggers Art. 26(7) obligation to inform workers and their representatives.
source: Deployer's determination

label: A serious incident has occurred involving the AI system
description: Whether a serious incident has occurred: an incident leading to death or serious harm to health, a serious and irreversible disruption of critical infrastructure, infringement of fundamental rights obligations, or serious property or environmental damage.
source: Provider's post-market monitoring; deployer's operational monitoring

label: AI system is placed on the EU market or put into service in the Union
description: Whether the provider places the AI system on the EU market or puts it into service within the Union, regardless of where the provider is established.
source: Provider's commercial and operational assessment

label: AI system is placed on the market together with a physical product
description: Whether the entity places a physical product on the EU market under their name or trademark and the AI system is an integral component of that product. Triggers product manufacturer obligations under Art. 2(1)(e).
source: Entity's product and commercialisation assessment

label: Primary purpose of the AI system

value_descriptions.general: The AI system is used for general commercial, administrative, or civil purposes not falling into any of the other categories.
value_descriptions.military: The AI system is used for military purposes, though not exclusively (it may also have civilian applications).
value_descriptions.military_exclusive: The AI system is used exclusively for military purposes with no civilian application. Triggers full exclusion from this Regulation under Art. 2(3).
value_descriptions.defence: The AI system is used for defence purposes, though not exclusively.
value_descriptions.defence_exclusive: The AI system is used exclusively for defence purposes. Triggers full exclusion from this Regulation under Art. 2(3).
value_descriptions.national_security_exclusive: The AI system is used exclusively for national security purposes. Triggers full exclusion from this Regulation under Art. 2(3). Note: if the same system is later repurposed for civilian use, this Regulation applies.
value_descriptions.law_enforcement_real_time_rbi: The AI system is a real-time remote biometric identification system deployed in publicly accessible spaces for law enforcement purposes. Subject to the near-total prohibition in Art. 5(1)(h), with narrow exceptions under Art. 5(2)-(7).

source: Provider's or deployer's assessment of the system's intended purpose and operational context
human_review_note: Scope.json and exemptions.json use slightly different value sets for military/defence exclusions (scope uses 'military','defence'; exemptions use 'military_exclusive','defence_exclusive'). Both sets are included in allowed_values. A reviewer should harmonise the conditions across scope.json and exemptions.json to use a single consistent set of values.

label: AI system is used solely for personal non-professional activity
description: Whether a natural person uses the AI system exclusively in the course of a purely personal non-professional activity. If true, deployer obligations do not apply under Art. 2(10), though the Art. 5 prohibited practices prohibition still applies.
source: Self-declaration by the natural person

label: AI system is developed and used solely for scientific research and development
description: Whether the AI system is developed and used exclusively for scientific R&D purposes before being placed on the market. If true, the Regulation does not apply. Note: testing in real-world conditions outside a laboratory (using actual data on real persons) is NOT excluded and is governed by Art. 57-63 (AI regulatory sandboxes).
source: Organisation's research mandate and operational assessment

label: AI system is legally authorised to detect, prevent, investigate or prosecute criminal offences
description: Whether the AI system is authorised by law to detect, prevent, investigate, or prosecute criminal offences. If true, the deployer is exempt from transparency disclosure obligations under Art. 50(1), (2), and (4), unless the system is also publicly accessible for crime reporting purposes.
source: Legal authorisation (national law or EU law empowering the system for law enforcement purposes)

label: It is obvious to a reasonably well-informed person that they are interacting with an AI system
description: Whether the AI interaction is obviously apparent from context to a reasonably well-informed natural person — e.g. a clearly branded AI chatbot interface. If true, the Art. 50(1) disclosure obligation does not apply.
source: Deployer's or provider's assessment of the interaction context

label: Date on which a serious incident was identified
description: The date on which the provider or deployer became aware of a serious incident. Used to compute the incident reporting deadlines: 2 days for death, 10 days for death or serious health damage, 15 days for other serious incidents (Art. 73).
source: Provider's or deployer's post-market monitoring and incident management system

label: Date the automatically generated log was created
description: The date on which the AI system automatically generated a specific log. Used to compute the minimum 6-month retention deadline for that log under Art. 26(6). Deployers must retain each log for at least 6 months from its generation date.
source: Deployer's log management system

label: Entity is established or located in the European Union
description: Whether the provider or deployer has their place of establishment or is located within the EU. Affects scope applicability and which provisions apply directly.
source: Entity registration / place of establishment

label: Outputs of the AI system are used in the European Union
description: Whether the output produced by the AI system is used in the Union, even if the provider or deployer is established outside the EU.
source: Operational assessment of where outputs are applied
